title: Domain/repository cutover: make group_ids[] the only API key grouping model
description:
Objective:
- Refactor API key domain structs and repository/query layer to use group_ids[] only.

Scope:
- Replace GroupID/GrantedGroups with GroupIDs/Groups in domain and repo-facing models.
- Update API key CRUD/read/list/search query logic.
- Remove repo methods tied to scalar group_id/api_key_groups.

Acceptance:
- No runtime API key domain object depends on scalar GroupID.

Risks:
- Hidden repository dependencies.

Verification:
- Compile passes.
- API key service/repo tests pass.
notes:
Domain/repository cutover continued in current working tree. Replaced core APIKey service/cache/repository surface to canonical group_ids[] + groups[] model and removed service-layer dependence on GroupID/GrantedGroups in key paths. internal/service now compiles.
Checkpoint 2026-04-10 regression/dead-code pass: removed the remaining service.APIKey compat fields `GroupID` and `Group`, tightened `EffectiveGroup`/`EffectiveGroupID`/`CanonicalGrantedGroups` to read only `GroupIDs` + `Groups`, switched API key list filtering from scalar `filters.GroupID` to canonical `filters.GroupIDs`, and updated in-scope gateway/ops/test call sites to use effective-group semantics only. Full `cd backend && go test ./...` is green after this pass.

title: Refactor API keys to group_ids[] first-class and remove api_key_groups
description:
Objective:
- Replace API key single-group legacy with group_ids[] as sole source of truth across persistence, auth, routing, billing, and admin UI.

Scope:
- Remove api_key_groups and api_keys.group_id.
- Introduce api_keys.group_ids[] canonical model.
- Remove GroupID/GrantedGroups compat in runtime.
- Add canonical effective-group resolution for execution endpoints.
- Update admin API/UI to use group_ids[] only.

Execution order:
1. sub2api-zb3 — schema cutover
2. sub2api-3i3 — domain/repository cutover
3. sub2api-wyr — auth snapshot/middleware cutover
4. sub2api-s21 — effective-group resolver
5. sub2api-9ir — gateway handler migration
6. sub2api-8ew — billing/subscription semantics cleanup
7. sub2api-u9e — admin API/frontend migration
8. sub2api-ps8 — regression sweep and dead-code removal

Acceptance:
- API keys persist only group_ids[].
- No runtime path reads scalar GroupID.
- Discovery endpoints union all groups.
- Execution endpoints resolve one effective group or fail explicit.
- No fallback/backward compatibility.

Risks:
- Breaking schema/runtime cutover.
- Hidden scalar-group dependencies in auth/billing/gateway layers.

Verification:
- Schema migration removes group_id and api_key_groups.
- Single-group via group_ids=[x] works.
- Multi-group resolves correct effective group by model family.
- Ambiguous/no-eligible requests fail explicit.

comments.author: mrcagents
comments.text: Execution-lane note: implement strictly in this order: 1) sub2api-zb3 schema cutover, 2) sub2api-3i3 domain/repository cutover, 3) sub2api-wyr auth snapshot/middleware cutover, 4) sub2api-s21 effective-group resolver, 5) sub2api-9ir gateway handler migration, 6) sub2api-8ew billing/subscription semantics cleanup, 7) sub2api-u9e admin API/frontend migration, 8) sub2api-ps8 regression sweep and dead-code removal. No fallback, no backward compatibility, no backfill.

notes:
Subagent started execution in mandated order. Beginning step 1/8: sub2api-zb3 schema cutover. Hard constraints honored: no fallback, no backward compatibility, no backfill.
Current state: step 1 exposed repo-wide hard dependency surface. Started 093 migration + ent schema cutover; compile now fails until downstream ordered steps are completed (service/auth/gateway/admin).
Progress: schema cutover remains in working tree; domain/repository and auth-cache slices partially migrated; internal/service package compiles after replacing GroupID/GrantedGroups hot paths with GroupIDs/Groups/EffectiveGroup. Continuing into gateway/admin/frontend compile break surface.
Checkpoint 2026-04-10: fresh full backend red-surface pass executed from current working tree with `cd backend && go test ./...`. Result: RED SURFACE IS NOW EMPTY at backend package-test level; command exited 0. All backend packages passed, including prior hotspots internal/handler, internal/server/routes, internal/repository, and internal/service. Current backend status is green across the full `./...` sweep, so there is no remaining backend failure cluster to classify from this checkpoint. This is a pure diagnosis pass only; no unrelated fixes were introduced in this step.
Checkpoint 2026-04-10 semantic GroupID sweep: full repo sweep from the existing working tree completed as an analysis-only pass. Classification model fixed to exactly three buckets: [A] must convert to GroupIds[] for API-key granted-group membership/discovery/admin payload surfaces; [B] must convert to effective-group semantics for request-time execution/routing/billing/sticky-session paths that must resolve one active group from group_ids[]; [C] truly unrelated scalar group domain and may remain scalar (subscriptions, redeem/invite/default-subscription settings, account_groups membership rows, ops/usage/history filters keyed by one realized request group, group->fallback references, user allowed-groups, and similar non-API-key-membership concepts). Key findings: runtime API-key membership storage and user-key UI are mostly already in [A]; the heaviest remaining [B] surface clusters in gateway_service/openai_gateway_service handler-call graphs where a resolved `*int64 groupID` is threaded as the single execution context; scalar `group_id` remnants outside API-key membership mostly land in [C], not migration debt. No code changes beyond bead checkpoint update in this note; constraints preserved: no fallback, no backward compatibility, no backfill.

title: [sub2api] Invite code entitlement v1 design lane
description: Invite code entitlement v1 for Sub2API. Business rules: every user created via invite code always gets concurrency = 1. Each invite code grants exactly one benefit type only: either balance or subscription. No mixed benefits and no concurrency reward type. Register flow remains the entry point via invitation_code. Validation: balance invite requires balance_amount > 0 and no subscription fields; subscription invite requires subscription_group_id + subscription_days > 0 and no balance field. Runtime apply flow: validate invite code -> create user -> set concurrency = 1 -> apply one benefit branch -> consume invite code -> write history/audit; all inside one DB transaction. Subscription semantics: if user already has the same subscription/group, extend expiry. Out of scope v1: redeem-after-register flow, referral reward tree, mixed grants, concurrency as selectable benefit type.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Admin can create invite code with benefit_type=balance. 2) Admin can create invite code with benefit_type=subscription. 3) Registering with invite code creates user with concurrency=1. 4) Balance invite adds correct balance and writes history. 5) Subscription invite grants correct group for correct days. 6) Invalid mixed payload (balance + subscription) is rejected. 7) Benefit apply + invite consume are transactional. 8) Admin UI clearly states concurrency=1 is fixed for invite-created users.
notes: Implementation plan v1: DB: extend invite code schema with benefit_type, balance_amount, subscription_group_id, subscription_days; enforce mutual exclusion validation. Backend: extend invite code CRUD validation, wire registration flow to apply exactly one benefit branch and hard-set concurrency=1 in transaction, reuse existing balance/subscription persistence/history paths where possible. Frontend admin: update invite code create/edit/list UI to choose one benefit type only and show a fixed note that invite-created users always get concurrency=1. Verify: API tests for create/register/invalid payload/extend semantics, plus admin UI smoke verification.

comments.author: mrcagents
comments.text: Spec final v1 recorded. This lane now uses the following source-of-truth: every invite-created user always gets concurrency=1, and each invite code grants exactly one benefit type only (balance or subscription). No mixed benefits. Next implementation should follow the DB/backend/frontend/verify breakdown captured in issue notes and acceptance criteria.

comments.author: mrcagents
comments.text: Implementation lane kicked off. Active execution now moves to a dedicated implementation issue covering DB migration, backend validation/apply flow, admin UI wiring, and verification for invite code entitlement v1.

title: [sub2api] Invite code entitlement v1 implementation lane
description: Implement invite code entitlement v1 from source-of-truth lane sub2api-6nk. Scope: DB schema/migration for mutually-exclusive benefit type (balance or subscription), backend CRUD validation, registration-time transactional apply flow with fixed concurrency=1 for invite-created users, admin UI create/edit/list support, and verification coverage. Execution breakdown: (1) DB migration + model fields, (2) backend validation and transactional grant application, (3) admin UI wiring, (4) tests and local verify, (5) local deploy smoke check if needed.

title: Billing/subscription semantics cleanup for group_ids[] model
description:
Objective:
- Align billing, subscription, and eligibility semantics with effective-group or aggregate-group behavior under group_ids[].

Scope:
- Execution-scope checks use resolved group.
- Aggregate endpoints operate across group_ids[].

Acceptance:
- No billing/subscription path assumes scalar GroupID.

Risks:
- Mixed semantics across endpoints.

Verification:
- Billing/subscription tests pass for single-group and multi-group cases.
notes: Strict canonical-only follow-up pass completed from the existing working tree, scoped only to the 5 remaining OpenAIGatewayService.RecordUsage failures. Exact code changes: (1) APIKey.EffectiveGroup now returns the first hydrated ordered groups[] entry even when group objects are not fully runtime-valid yet, so record-usage semantics still honor canonical group_ids[] ordering instead of collapsing to nil; (2) OpenAI usage logging now persists upstream_model from the raw trimmed upstream metadata field directly, preserving equal-valued requested/upstream model strings instead of suppressing them via a helper; (3) post-apply billing side effects remain on the real billing path restored in applyUsageBilling. Exact files changed in this pass: backend/internal/service/api_key.go, backend/internal/service/openai_gateway_service.go. Tests run: `go test ./internal/service -run 'TestOpenAIGatewayServiceRecordUsage_(UsesUserSpecificGroupRate|FallsBackToGroupDefaultRateOnResolverError|FallsBackToGroupDefaultRateWhenResolverMissing|UsesRequestedModelAndUpstreamModelMetadataFields|SubscriptionBillingSetsSubscriptionFields)' -count=1` => PASS; `go test ./internal/service -run 'TestOpenAIGatewayServiceRecordUsage' -count=1` => PASS. Result: the 5 remaining failures are eliminated with no fallback branch, no compat hack branch, and no test-only conditionals added in RecordUsage logic.

title: Gateway handler migration: remove scalar GroupID reads from execution paths
description:
Objective:
- Refactor gateway/provider handlers to resolve effective group from group_ids[] instead of reading apiKey.GroupID.

Scope:
- OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Antigravity execution paths.
- Selection/routing calls must use resolved group only.

Acceptance:
- No execution handler reads apiKey.GroupID.
- Requests route by effective group semantics.

Risks:
- Endpoint regressions across providers.

Verification:
- Execution endpoint integration tests pass for single-group and multi-group keys.
notes: Handler cleanup continued in current working tree. Narrow pass migrated openai_chat_completions, gateway chat_completions, gateway responses, gateway messages/count_tokens/models/usage paths from scalar apiKey.GroupID/apiKey.Group reads toward EffectiveGroup()/EffectiveGroupID() and group_ids logging. Follow-up narrow pass for openai gateway handler default_mapped_model resolution is now green: backend/internal/handler/openai_gateway_handler_test.go aligns TestResolveOpenAIForwardDefaultMappedModel with canonical GroupIDs/Groups/EffectiveGroup semantics via hydrated active openai groups, including explicit coverage that GroupIDs order decides the effective group. Targeted handler test passes and full backend rerun still fails next at package github.com/Wei-Shaw/sub2api/internal/service (package-level red surface not yet narrowed to a single failing test in this pass).

title: Regression sweep and dead-code removal for legacy single-group API key logic
description:
Objective:
- Remove all legacy compat remnants and verify no scalar-group assumptions remain.

Scope:
- Delete helpers/compat wrappers around GroupID/GrantedGroups/api_key_groups.
- Add regression tests and grep-based verification.

Acceptance:
- No runtime references to GroupID/GrantedGroups/api_key_groups remain.
- Single-group via group_ids=[x] and multi-group both behave correctly.

Risks:
- Hidden references in secondary modules/tests.

Verification:
- Grep verification clean.
- Full regression suite passes.
notes:
Fresh full-backend regression checkpoint captured from the existing working tree. Ran `cd backend && go test ./...` after the 5 RecordUsage failures were cleaned. Result: full backend test sweep is green with exit code 0; no failing backend package remains to classify at this checkpoint. This advances regression visibility for the canonical group_ids[] cutover, but dead-code/grep cleanup work is still open until legacy scalar-group remnants are explicitly removed and verified.
Checkpoint 2026-04-10 narrow cleanup pass: deleted the remaining in-scope `service.APIKey` scalar compat remnants (`GroupID`, `Group`) and converted in-scope request/filter/logging paths plus affected tests to canonical `group_ids[]`/`groups[]` + `EffectiveGroup()`/`EffectiveGroupID()` semantics. Grep sweep results: no `GrantedGroups` remain in runtime code under the API-key lane; `api_key_groups` remains only in migration/history/schema-verification files (`091`, `093`, migration schema test) which are intentionally kept; `default_group_id` / `granted_group_ids` remnants remain in auth/invite/public-contract surfaces outside this packet scope. Full `cd backend && go test ./...` passes after the cleanup pass, so the packet is ready for broader validation from the current backend state.
Production-readiness checkpoint 2026-04-10: verification-first pass completed from current working tree. Ran `cd backend && go test ./...` => PASS (full backend green). Ran `cd frontend && pnpm build` => PASS; only non-blocking Vite chunk-size warnings remained. Final semantic grep sweep for `group_id`, `granted_group_ids`, `default_group_id`, `api_key_groups` classified residue as follows: [1] blocking API-key membership residue still remains in the current in-flight API-key surface, most notably user keys filter/request contract still exposing scalar `group_id` in `frontend/src/api/keys.ts` and `frontend/src/views/user/KeysView.vue`; [2] harmless/unrelated scalar hits remain in subscription/redeem/ops/usage/group-fallback/account-group domains where one realized group or non-API-key entity is still the correct model; [3] expected historical/schema references remain in migration/test coverage for `api_key_groups`. Assessment: backend/frontend verification is green, but working tree is not yet production-ready to recommend commit/push for the canonical API-key cutover because the user API-key filter contract still leaks legacy scalar `group_id` semantics instead of a canonical `group_ids[]`/effective-group story. Recommend one more narrow cleanup pass to eliminate that residue before commit/push.
Checkpoint 2026-04-10 exact continuation pass: reran `cd backend && go test ./...` from the current working tree after the canonical-storage refactor and got a fresh green backend checkpoint again; command exited 0 with all backend packages passing, including `internal/repository`, `internal/service`, `internal/handler`, `internal/server/routes`, and `internal/setup`. Then ran a strict grep sweep for runtime-facing residue of `api_key_groups` and legacy scalar API-key group semantics outside docs/history. Result: no runtime code path still references `api_key_groups`; remaining hits are confined to intentional migration/schema verification surfaces (`backend/migrations/091_add_api_key_groups.sql`, `backend/migrations/093_api_keys_group_ids_array_cutover.sql`, `backend/internal/repository/migrations_schema_integration_test.go`). Narrow residue actually removed in this pass: the leftover user-facing API-key create test harness in `frontend/src/components/__tests__/ApiKeyCreate.spec.ts` was converted from legacy scalar `group_id` request semantics to canonical `group_ids[]` semantics (`[]` for default/unbound, `[1]` for one membership group). After this pass, the previously identified user runtime-facing residue in `frontend/src/api/keys.ts` / `frontend/src/views/user/KeysView.vue` is already canonical `group_ids[]`; the remaining `group_id` / `default_group_id` / `subscription_group_id` hits in repo grep are classified as unrelated scalar domains or explicit effective-group/result fields, not leftover API-key membership storage semantics. Constraints preserved: no fallback, no backward compatibility, no backfill.

title: Introduce canonical effective-group resolver for request-time routing
description:
Objective:
- Add one canonical request-time effective group resolver for execution endpoints and union semantics for discovery endpoints.

Scope:
- Implement ResolveEffectiveGroupForRequest.
- Define explicit errors for no eligible group / ambiguous group.

Acceptance:
- Execution endpoints resolve exactly one effective group or fail explicit.
- Discovery endpoints union all groups.

Risks:
- Ambiguous endpoint semantics if not classified correctly.

Verification:
- Resolver unit tests cover openai/anthropic/gemini/antigravity and ambiguity cases.
notes: Analysis-only checkpoint from the current working tree: completed a repo semantic GroupID sweep to separate true effective-group work from unrelated scalar group domains. Resolver-relevant [B] bucket confirmed across request-time execution paths: middleware resolveEffectiveGroup, gateway/openai/gemini handlers, sticky-session cache keys, account selection/routing, billing eligibility checks, usage pricing writes that stamp one realized request group, and service methods that currently thread a single `groupID *int64` execution context. Discovery/admin membership [A] and unrelated scalar group domains [C] were explicitly separated so resolver work can stay narrow and avoid false-positive churn. No implementation in this pass; this note only records classification evidence for the upcoming canonical resolver lane under strict no-fallback / no-backward-compat / no-backfill constraints.

title: Admin API and frontend migration to group_ids[] only
description:
Objective:
- Move admin/user API key create/update/list/detail flows to group_ids[] only.

Scope:
- Replace group_id/granted_group_ids payloads and UI state with group_ids[].
- Update list/detail rendering.

Acceptance:
- Admin/UI only speaks group_ids[].

Risks:
- UI/API contract breaking changes.

Verification:
- Create/edit/list API key flows pass in UI/API tests.
notes: Current narrow pass completed the user-facing/frontend slice needed for compile: frontend/src/views/user/KeysView.vue now uses group_ids[] for form/edit/change-group and Select binding; frontend/src/components/admin/user/UserApiKeysModal.vue now reads primary group from group_ids[]; frontend/src/types/index.ts updated ApiKey/CreateApiKeyRequest/UpdateApiKeyRequest toward group_ids[] and retained only minimal group field for in-flight callers. frontend pnpm build is green. Follow-up narrow backend admin pass now moved PUT /api/v1/admin/api-keys/:id request body from legacy group_id to canonical group_ids[] (nil=no-op, []=unbind, [x]=bind exactly one group) and cleaned admin handler tests/stub expectations accordingly. dto.APIKeyFromService now preserves canonical GroupIDs even when hydrated Groups are absent, matching in-flight admin update responses. Remaining red is broader backend suite fallout beyond this admin API key update path.

title: Auth snapshot and middleware cutover to group_ids[]/groups[]
description:
Objective:
- Make auth cache snapshot and middleware expose only group_ids[] and groups[].

Scope:
- Replace GroupID/Group/GrantedGroups in auth snapshot.
- Update middleware/context/invalidation to use canonical multi-group state.

Acceptance:
- Request auth context exposes GroupIDs and Groups only.
- No GrantedGroups compat path remains.

Risks:
- Cache invalidation and stale snapshot issues.

Verification:
- Auth middleware/cache tests pass.
notes: Auth cache snapshot/middleware cutover underway. API key auth cache snapshot now stores group_ids[] + groups[] only; service-layer snapshot hydration updated accordingly.

title: Schema cutover: replace api_keys.group_id/api_key_groups with api_keys.group_ids[]
description:
Objective:
- Perform schema-level cutover so api_keys.group_ids[] becomes the only persistence model for API key group binding.

Scope:
- Add api_keys.group_ids.
- Remove api_keys.group_id.
- Remove api_key_groups.
- Update migration/schema tests accordingly.

Acceptance:
- group_id and api_key_groups no longer exist after migration.
- group_ids[] exists and is required.

Risks:
- Breaking DB/schema change.

Verification:
- Migration applies cleanly.
- Schema integration tests pass.

comments.author: mrcagents
comments.text: Schema cutover note updated: migration 093 now follows explicit data-migration-before-drop semantics. Sequence is: add api_keys.group_ids -> migrate data from api_key_groups and api_keys.group_id into canonical array -> dedupe/sort -> verify no empty group_ids remain -> only then drop api_key_groups and api_keys.group_id. This is explicit data migration, not runtime fallback/backward compatibility.

comments.author: mrcagents
comments.text: Execution packet continuation consumed the revised 093 strategy as authoritative in the in-flight plan. Current rule is locked: migrate-data-before-drop only. Downstream repo/service/handler work continues under strict constraints: no fallback path, no backward compatibility branch, no runtime backfill. Verification note tightened too: schema assertions now explicitly require the post-cutover state (canonical api_keys.group_ids[] + GIN index present, legacy api_key_groups and api_keys.group_id absent only after migration/verification sequence).

comments.author: assistant
comments.text: Strict canonical refactor continuation applied on the existing working tree. Migration 093 was rewritten again to the final approved principle: only api_keys.group_ids is canonical, migration rewrites from legacy api_keys.group_id only, and api_key_groups is removed rather than used as canonical or fallback input. Repository pass removed the in-flight join-table hydration workaround from api_key_repo, so GetByID/GetByKey/GetByKeyForAuth/List/Search now read api_keys.group_ids directly. In-scope tests/fixtures were updated to construct API keys with GroupIDs only, and schema test wording now matches rewrite-then-drop semantics. Verification status from this continuation: service tests targeted at invite-login/API-key surface pass, but repository integration suite is currently blocked in this environment because testcontainers cannot obtain a Docker client. Remaining blocker is broader repo/test residue outside the edited slice that still references legacy scalar/service fields or historical migration files, not a new workaround introduced by this pass.

notes: Authoritative cutover strategy for this packet is now explicit and consumed by execution: migration 093 must add api_keys.group_ids, rewrite canonical membership from legacy api_keys.group_id during cutover, verify no empty canonical memberships remain, and only then drop api_key_groups plus api_keys.group_id. Final approved principle for the current continuation is stricter than the earlier note: after cutover there is no canonical source other than api_keys.group_ids, no join-table hydration path, no runtime compat path, no fallback logic, and no backfill-at-request-time. Current working tree/backend lanes must assume post-cutover schema only.
